rationale: The citing paper uses Danaher, Bonfrer, and Dhar (2008) as prior evidence that competitor advertising can create interference and dilute a focal message. That idea is then tied to the paper's design logic: they include a control debunking message that adds competitive clutter but does not correct misinformation, so they can isolate the incremental effect of debunking content. Even though the citation appears in a grouped set of prior studies, it plays a meaningful role in framing and identification rather than a purely passing mention.
relation_description: The citing study applies the competitive advertising interference insight from Danaher et al. (2008) to a misinformation-debunking context. It uses that interference/clutter logic to motivate an experimental control that separates generic competitive message clutter from true debunking effects.
